title: Global Stock Rally Powers On as Chip Shares Surge: Markets Wrap
summary: Global equities extended their record rally as momentum in technology shares accelerated and geopolitical tensions eased. Oil slipped.

title: FTSE 100 Futures Fall as Oil Drops and the Pound Rises
Jefferies Says Investors Boost ‘Nuclear Exposure’: ESG Investing
summary: Almost two-thirds of fund managers permit some level of “nuclear exposure,” with 34% allowing investments in nuclear weaponry, according to Jefferies Financial Group Inc.’s fourth-annual ESG and defense survey.

title: EU Chamber on Business Confidence in China
summary: Jens Eskelund, President of the European Union Chamber of Commerce in China, tells Bloomberg’s The China Snow that the business group's latest survey shows an "inflection point" in confidence. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Everybody Wants Fed Independence, Rubenstein Says
summary: Carlyle Co-Founder and Co-Chairman David Rubenstein discusses his outlook for interest rates and his expectations of the Federal Reserve under Chair Kevin Warsh. He also shares his views on investing with Stephen Engle from the sidelines of the UBS Asian Investment Conference in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: HK-Traded Insilico Medicine Is Said to Explore UAE Listing
summary: Insilico Medicine Cayman TopCo is considering a second listing in Abu Dhabi, according to people familiar with the matter, which would make it the first company from outside of the Gulf to list shares on a regional exchange.

title: Gold Holds Loss as Traders Weigh Prospects for US-Iran Deal
summary: Gold held a loss, as fresh clashes in the Persian Gulf weighed on hopes for a US-Iran peace deal and reinforced concerns that inflation could keep interest rates higher for longer.

title: Ex-Fed Official Evans on What's Next for Monetary Policy
summary:
Former Chicago Fed President Charles Evans discusses what's next for monetary policy.
Evans, who ran the Chicago Fed for 16 years until 2023, emphasizes that monetary policy ahead will likely remain cautious, balancing inflation risks with growth concerns. He speaks with Stephen Engle from the sidelines of the 'UBS Asian Investment Conference' in Hong Kong. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SK Hynix, Micron Join $1 Trillion Market Cap Club | The Asia Trade 5/27/2026
summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Adnoc Exports Another LNG Shipment Through Hormuz to India
summary: Another tanker carrying liquefied natural gas from Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. has exited the Strait of Hormuz, adding to a recent uptick in energy flows through the vital waterway.

title: ASX Shares Eye Decade Low After Cost Woes Fuel Profit Downgrades
summary: ASX Ltd. shares have tumbled to their lowest level in almost a decade after rising costs prompted several brokers to trim their earnings outlooks for Australia’s beleaguered exchange operator.

title: Defense Billionaire’s Father Builds New Business on CSG Model
summary: The founder of CSG NV, which completed the biggest ever initial public offering for a pure defense company four months ago, is seeking to raise as much as €200 million ($233 million) this year to expand his industrial group.

title: Korean Stocks Surge 100% in 2026 to Surpass Dotcom Era Gains
summary: The breathtaking rally in South Korean stocks took gains for 2026 to 100%, eclipsing even the historic run-ups seen before the dotcom bubble burst and during the nation’s industrial boom in the late 1980s.

title: Oil Dips as US Sees Progress in Iran Deal Despite Fresh Tensions
summary: Oil dropped on optimism that the US and Iran will reach a peace deal despite fresh hostilities and uncertainty over the vital Strait of Hormuz.

title: Japan’s 40-Year Bond Sale Draws Strong Demand on Higher Yields
summary: Japan’s 40-year government bond auction drew stronger demand than its 12-month average, as higher yields attracted investors despite inflation concerns driven by the Middle East conflict.

title: Samsung Workers Accept Wage Deal That Averts Chip Plant Strike
summary: Samsung Electronics Co. union members voted in favor of a compensation deal that will hand chip workers an average bonus of about $340,000, staving off a strike that threatened to disrupt global chip supply.

title: Thailand Eyes $5 Billion From Notes, Loans as Bond Yields Soar
summary: Thailand plans to raise about $5 billion through a mix of promissory notes and term loans to fund a raft of measures to ease living costs, shunning bonds after the Iran war sent sovereign yields to multi-month highs.

title: The Chips Are All-In for the Trillion-Dollar Club
summary: Boom makes Micron and SK Hynix the newest members.

title: Global Aluminum Rally Could Draw Record Exports From China
summary: The rally in the global aluminum market could spur record exports of the metal from China, where elevated prices are capping consumption.

title: Memory Chip Frenzy Sends SK Hynix, Micron Into $1 Trillion Club
summary: The breakneck surge in memory-chip stocks is intensifying, sending the market capitalizations of SK Hynix Inc. and Micron Technology Inc. above $1 trillion for the first time, as investors bet the AI boom will lead to a sustained revaluation of the industry.

title: UBS' Khan on China Business, AI Impact and Middle East (Full Interview)
summary: UBS Group AG’s Asia Pacific President Iqbal Khan discusses the China market, potential impact of AI on jobs and how the bank is navigating geopolitical turmoil. He speaks with Bloomberg's Stephen Engle in Hong Kong. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Base Metals Advance as US Touts Progress Toward Iran Peace Deal
summary: Copper climbed and aluminum was on track for its highest close in four years on cautious optimism that the US and Iran could reach a peace deal, despite a fresh flare-up of hostilities in the Persian Gulf.

title: UBS’ Khan Says AI Will Impact Jobs While Aiding Productivity
summary: UBS Group AG’s Asia Pacific President Iqbal Khan said artificial intelligence will free up capacity and improve productivity but also have an impact on jobs.

title: China Bond Bears Eye Niche Swap Trade as Rally Doubts Grow
summary: Chinese investors are betting a months-long rally in two-year government bonds has gone too far, turning to arbitrage trades more commonly used in the US and Europe to put that view to work.

title: Astellas Pharma Shares Fall After Unveiling Five-Year Plan
summary: Astellas Pharma Inc. shares fell to the lowest in about four months after the Tokyo-based drugmaker’s five-year plan offered few surprises.

title: EU Firms Warm to China Even as Tensions Spiral Over Export Surge
summary: European firms in China are turning more upbeat about their business outlook, a new survey found, just as Brussels appears poised to take action against a swelling trade imbalance with Beijing.

title: Insurance Mogul Lindberg Gets 12 Years for $2 Billion Fraud
summary: North Carolina investment firm founder Greg Lindberg was sentenced Tuesday to 12 years in prison for siphoning more than $2 billion in reserves backing insurance policies and using the proceeds to pay for jets, mansions and a 214-foot yacht.

title: Iqbal Khan on Geopolitics, AI, and Growth
summary: UBS Asia Pacific Co-President of Global Wealth Management Iqbal Khan speaks to Bloomberg's Stephen Engle as the UBS Asian Investment Conference kicks off in Hong Kong. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Touts Iran Deal Prospects Amid Fresh Hormuz Tensions
summary: The US is touting progress toward a peace deal with Iran to end the nearly three-month war, despite fresh hostilities and uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg's Derek Wallbank has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)
